perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx with the Product free from defect and meeting the product specification attached to this Agreement as Annex A (the "Product Specifications").
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer shall use its best efforts to supply Ultragenyx with the Product meeting the product specification attached to this Agreement as Annex A (the "Product Specifications").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'shall supply... free from defect' with 'shall use its best efforts to supply,' making performance less binding and removing the guarantee against defects. It contradicts Article 4(3), where Cremer 'represents and warrants that all quantities of Product delivered under the Agreement were manufactured in accordance with GMP. The Product shall be free from defects if it is within the specifications according to Annex A'. This creates an in-text contradiction between Article 1 and Article 4 regarding the guarantee of defect-free products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Cremer represents and warrants that all quantities of Product delivered under the Agreement were manufactured in accordance with GMP. The Product shall be free from defects if it is within the specifications according to Annex A.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that Cremer 'shall supply... free from defect,' which created a guarantee of product quality. The changed text, replacing this with 'shall use its best efforts to supply,' introduces ambiguity and weakens the guarantee. This directly contradicts the statement in the Contradicted Text: 'The Product shall be free from defects if it is within the specifications according to Annex A.' By removing the guarantee of being 'free from defect' and replacing it with 'best efforts', the contract now presents conflicting statements regarding product quality, fitting the definition of an Ambiguity leading to an In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The prices payable by Ultragenyx to Cremer for the Product (the "Price") shall be agreed [***] every contract year; provided, that the Price may not increase more than the [***] for such period or [***]%, whichever is higher. At the date of signing the Parties agree on a Price of €[***] per kilogram for the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: The prices payable by Ultragenyx to Cremer for the Product (the "Price") will be reviewed and potentially adjusted [***] every contract year. The Parties aim to keep price increases reasonable, considering market conditions. At the date of signing the Parties agree on a Price of €[***] per kilogram for the Product, but this price is subject to change based on material costs.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing the fixed Price increase formula with a subjective 'reasonable' increase based on 'market conditions' and explicitly stating that the initial price is subject to change based on material costs, removing the previously defined price limit. This contradicts the intended nature of the contract which stated that the Parties need to agree on a fixed price every contract year. If the parties cannot come to an agreement, Cremer can choose not to deliver, but this perturbation creates an inconsistency by adding 'this price is subject to change based on material costs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the parties cannot agree on a price for the Product by the beginning of a following contract year, Cremer may refuse to deliver the Product to Ultragenyx until the Parties agreed on a respective price.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the previously defined fixed price increase formula and replaces it with a subjective 'reasonable' increase based on 'market conditions'. It also explicitly states that the initial price is subject to change based on material costs, contradicting the original intention that the Parties need to agree on a fixed price every contract year. This introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states 'If the parties cannot agree on a price for the Product by the beginning of a following contract year, Cremer may refuse to deliver the Product to Ultragenyx until the Parties agreed on a respective price,' because the 'price is subject to change based on material costs' which is inconsistent with this section. This constitutes an 'Ambiguity - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: If a Party materially breaches an obligation under this Agreement and does not cure such breach within sixty (60) days of receiving notice of such breach from the non-breaching Party, the non-breaching Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the breaching Party.
perturbation.changed_text: If a Party fails to substantially perform its obligations under this Agreement and does not demonstrate a good faith effort to remedy such failure within a reasonable time frame after receiving notice from the non-performing Party, the non-performing Party may seek equitable remedies, such as specific performance, but termination will only be considered as a last resort.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the termination clause. Replacing 'materially breaches' with 'fails to substantially perform' and introducing 'good faith effort to remedy' makes the threshold for breach vaguer. Furthermore, restricting the remedy to 'equitable remedies' and making termination a 'last resort' removes the non-breaching party's right to immediately terminate for a clear breach. This introduces a contradiction because Article 5(3) states that 'Every termination has to be in writing'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Every termination has to be in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for immediate termination upon written notice for a material breach. The changed text replaces 'materially breaches' with 'fails to substantially perform' and introduces 'good faith effort to remedy', making the threshold for breach vaguer. Furthermore, restricting the remedy to 'equitable remedies' and making termination a 'last resort' removes the non-breaching party's right to immediately terminate for a clear breach. The contradicted text states 'Every termination has to be in writing'. The change to the text weakens the ability to terminate, even if in writing, as it's now only a 'last resort', thus contradicting the idea that termination can occur based on the original grounds, introducing ambiguity and hindering the enforceability of termination.
